title: HPE Morpheus

Technical brief

[第2页]
Figure 1. Migration dashboard showing a migration ready to run
Feature overview
environments to HPE virtual machine (HVM) clusters, built on a robust
experience. In addition, it seamlessly integrates with existing environments,
allowing efficient bulk migrations and supporting up to 20 VMs in parallel
HPE Morpheus VM Essentials Software  |  2
Customer benefits
• Reduced complexity:
• Operational efficiency:
VMware licensing
and container-native workloads

[第3页]
new HPE ecosystem without manual reconfiguration.
Figure 2. Migration dashboard showing the history of migrated VMs
Key capabilities

— Cross-platform support: Compatible with major Linux distributions
(Red Hat®, Ubuntu, SUSE, and such) and Windows Server 2022+

— Integrated workflow: Fully managed through the VM Essentials
UI—create, configure, and implement migration plans with intuitive steps

HPE Morpheus VM Essentials Software  |  3

[第4页]
Figure 3. Creating a migration plan—mapping resources
HPE Morpheus VM Essentials Software  |  4
Built-in validation checks
the migration before it happens and then validate as the migration progresses:

— Confirms VirtIO / VirtIO-SCSI support and presence of QEMU guest tools

— Verifies VM power state and network reachability

— Checks datastore capacity for thick-provisioned storage

— Detects unsupported configurations such as attached ISOs/CD-ROMs

— Validates OS compatibility and driver readiness
Workflow and failure handling
Migration workflow overview
The migration workflow for transitioning VMs from VMware vCenter to an

[第5页]

— Plan creation: Define source VMware vCenter and add it as a cloud to VM
Essentials, set the target HVM cluster, and group ownership.

— VM selection: Choose one or more VMs to migrate.

— Resource mapping: Assign destination networks and datastores.

— Preparation phase: Validate guest OS tools are installed (VirtIO drivers /
QEMU guest agent), adjust mount points, and detach ISOs.

— Implementation phase: Initiate migration—the VM is powered off,
exported, converted, and powered on in the HVM hypervisor.

— Postmigration validation: Install guest agent (if not skipped) and finalize VM.
Figure 4. Reviewing migration plan before implementation
HPE Morpheus VM Essentials Software  |  5

[第6页]
Failure handling
to handling failures throughout the workflow.

— Source VM remains untouched in VMware vCenter.

— No destructive operations—original VM can be restarted and reused.

System requirements

— HVM hosts must run agent version 2.10.0 or higher.

— Hosts must have network access to VMware ESXi™ and vCenter.

— Source VMs must be running during the preparation phase.

— VirtIO and VirtIO-SCSI must be supported by source VMs.

— Destination datastore must have sufficient thick-provisioned space.

— ISOs and CD-ROMs must be detached prior to migration.

— Supported OS: Major Linux distributions and Windows Server 2022+.
